post.description:
Upload binary content + metadata as multipart/form-data. The backend writes content to disk, then emits yield:create on the bus; Stower persists it and returns a 202 with the new resourceId. Full reconciliation happens via SSE domain events (yield:created).

Generation workers and the /know/compose page both hit this route — bytes always travel on the HTTP wire, never on the bus.

format.description: Media type of the content (e.g. text/plain, text/markdown, image/png)

storageUri.description: Where the content has been / should be placed (file://... for local). If omitted, derived from name + format.

entityTypes.description: JSON-stringified array of entity type names

creationMethod.description: How this resource was created (api, ui, upload, reference, cli, clone, generated)

sourceAnnotationId.description: For AI-generated resources: the annotation that triggered generation. Nested into generatedFrom.annotationId on the persisted event.

sourceResourceId.description: For AI-generated resources: the source resource the generating annotation lives on. Nested into generatedFrom.resourceId on the persisted event.

generationPrompt.description: For AI-generated resources: the prompt that drove generation

generator.description: For AI-generated resources: JSON-stringified Agent (single object or array), capturing which model/worker produced the content

isDraft.description: 'true' or 'false' — whether the resource is a draft

400.description: Missing required fields (name, file, or format)
